<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Excellent! Now I have a very thorough understanding of the codebase. Let me create a comprehensive plan for building the Battle Royale game. This is a substantial task with many files to create.

Key differences between Battle Royale and Mindshot:
1. Battle Royale uses a square grid (8x8, 10x10, or 12x12) vs Mindshot's hexagonal grid
2. Battle Royale has 4 cardinal directions vs Mindshot's 6 hex directions
3. Battle Royale has configurable arena size and planning timer
4. Battle Royale has a "Command Queue" D-pad interaction pattern vs Mindshot's separate direction pickers


5. Battle Royale implements server-driven phase resolution with timing delays rather than frame-based resolution, and tracks additional stats like damageDealt, damageTaken, roundsSurvived, eliminations, and placements
6. The zone shrinks inward from the borders based on distance from edges, and the PRD confirms this server-driven timing approach in Section 12
7. Mindshot actually uses frame-based resolution with host-side auto-advancement via useEffect rather than server timers, so I'll adapt that pattern for Battle Royale's sequential phases while keeping server-driven advancement

Now I'm weighing how to handle the planning timer—Battle Royale needs a countdown with defaults when time expires, unlike Mindshot which just waits for player submissions. I'm considering whether to implement this server-side or client-side.

The PRD specifies server-driven resolution timing, which points toward using PartyKit's alarm API, but the current codebase doesn't use alarms. A simpler approach would be having the host manage the planning timer locally and signal when it expires, letting the server default any missing actions—this mirrors how Mindshot's host drives progression. But looking back at the PRD requirements, I need to handle both early resolution when all players lock in and fallback defaults when the timer expires, so I should probably implement this more robustly on the server side.

For the resolution phases, I'll have the host auto-advance through each phase based on animation durations, similar to Mindshot's frame progression. Now I'm mapping out the file structure I need to create: types for all the game state and actions, the zone shrinking algorithm, the resolution logic for handling moves and damage, and the core game state management. Building out the UI components...

Now I need to wire everything together—exporting from the game logic package, adding the server handler, and registering the game in the web app. Looking at how the PRD structures resolution, I should model Battle Royale similarly to Mindshot by pre-computing all the resolution steps upfront into a sequence that maps to the phases: movement, then shooting, then zone damage. The host can then step through these frames to animate the action.</details></think>

```json
{
  "plan": "# Build Battle Royale Game\n\nThe Battle Royale game is a turn-based tactical grid game for 2-4 players with a shrinking danger zone. It follows the same architecture as Mindshot (the closest existing game): pure logic reducer in &#96;game-logic&#96;, server message routing, host TV display + player phone controller.\n\n## Architecture\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    subgraph gameLogic [\"packages/game-logic (pure)\"]\n        types[\"types.ts - State, Messages, Position\"]\n        zone[\"zone.ts - Zone shrink algorithm\"]\n        resolution[\"resolution.ts - Move/Shoot/Damage resolution\"]\n        logic[\"logic.ts - createInitial + applyMessage reducer\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph server [\"apps/server\"]\n        serverTs[\"server.ts - Route battle-royale messages\"]\n    end\n\n    subgraph web [\"apps/web\"]\n        landing[\"app/games/battle-royale/page.tsx\"]\n        hostPage[\"app/games/battle-royale/host/page.tsx\"]\n        playerPage[\"app/games/battle-royale/player/page.tsx\"]\n        hostComp[\"BattleRoyaleHost.tsx - Grid + HUD + animations\"]\n        playerComp[\"BattleRoyalePlayer.tsx - CommandQueue + DPad\"]\n    end\n\n    logic --&gt; serverTs\n    serverTs --&gt;|sync| hostComp\n    serverTs --&gt;|sync| playerComp\n    playerComp --&gt;|submit_actions| serverTs\n    hostComp --&gt;|advance_phase / end_planning| serverTs\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Key Design Decisions\n\n- **Resolution model**: Like Mindshot's frame system -- &#96;resolveRound()&#96; produces an array of &#96;ResolutionStep&#96; snapshots (move1, move2, shoot, zone, summary). The host auto-advances through them with delays matching the PRD (1.5s for moves, 2.5s for shoot, 2.0s for zone).\n- **Planning timer**: Host-driven. The host counts down locally and sends &#96;end_planning&#96; when timer expires. Server defaults missing actions to Stay/Stay/Skip.\n- **Zone shrink**: Picks cells from outermost ring of remaining safe cells. Shrink count scales with arena: 6 (8x8), 9 (10x10), 13 (12x12).\n- **No collision**: Multiple players can occupy the same cell (per PRD).\n- **Simultaneous shooting**: All shots calculated from post-movement positions before damage applied.\n\n## Files to Create\n\n### Game Logic (&#96;packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/&#96;)\n\n- **[types.ts](packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/types.ts)** -- All types from PRD Section 11: &#96;BattleRoyaleGameState&#96;, &#96;BattleRoyalePlayer&#96;, &#96;PlayerActions&#96;, &#96;Phase&#96;, &#96;Direction&#96;, &#96;CellState&#96;, &#96;ArenaConfig&#96;, &#96;RoundEvent&#96;, &#96;BattleRoyaleMessage&#96;, &#96;ResolutionStep&#96;.\n- **[zone.ts](packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/zone.ts)** -- &#96;computeZoneUpdate(grid, shrinkCount)&#96;: promote warning-&gt;danger, pick new warning cells from outermost safe ring.\n- **[resolution.ts](packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/resolution.ts)** -- &#96;resolveRound(state)&#96;: produce &#96;ResolutionStep[]&#96; covering move1, move2, shoot, zone, eliminations. Returns the full state after resolution plus all events.\n- **[logic.ts](packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/logic.ts)** -- &#96;createInitialBattleRoyaleState(playerIds, arena, planningDuration)&#96;, &#96;applyBattleRoyaleMessage(state, msg, senderId?)&#96;. Messages: &#96;start_game&#96;, &#96;submit_actions&#96;, &#96;unlock_actions&#96;, &#96;end_planning&#96;, &#96;advance_phase&#96;, &#96;play_again&#96;.\n- **[index.ts](packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/index.ts)** -- Re-exports.\n- **[__tests__/logic.test.ts](packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/__tests__/logic.test.ts)** -- Approval tests covering: initial state, start_game, submit_actions, zone shrink, shooting hits/misses, movement off-grid, elimination, mutual elimination draw, end_planning defaults.\n\n### Server\n\n- **[apps/server/src/server.ts](apps/server/src/server.ts)** -- Add Battle Royale to imports, &#96;ServerState.game&#96; union, &#96;isBattleRoyaleMessage()&#96;, &#96;handleBattleRoyaleMessage()&#96;. Pattern follows Mindshot handler (lines 360-387).\n\n### Frontend Pages\n\n- **[apps/web/app/games/battle-royale/page.tsx](apps/web/app/games/battle-royale/page.tsx)** -- Landing page. Grid hero animation with colored tokens + shrinking red border. \"Host Battle Royale\" / \"Join a Game\" buttons. \"How to Play\" steps. Follows Mindshot landing page pattern.\n- **[apps/web/app/games/battle-royale/host/page.tsx](apps/web/app/games/battle-royale/host/page.tsx)** -- &#96;usePartyRoom&lt;BattleRoyaleGameState&gt;(roomCode, { asHost: true, gameType: 'battle-royale' })&#96;. Renders &#96;BattleRoyaleHost&#96;.\n- **[apps/web/app/games/battle-royale/player/page.tsx](apps/web/app/games/battle-royale/player/page.tsx)** -- &#96;usePartyRoom&lt;BattleRoyaleGameState&gt;(roomCode, { sessionKeyPrefix: 'lobby' })&#96;. Renders &#96;BattleRoyalePlayer&#96;.\n\n### Frontend Components (&#96;apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/&#96;)\n\n- **[BattleRoyaleHost.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/BattleRoyaleHost.tsx)** -- Main host component. States: lobby (QR + room code + settings: arena size, timer duration), planning (grid + timer + player HUD + ready indicators), resolution (animated grid with phase label auto-advancing), game-over (winner announcement + stats). Grid rendered as CSS grid or SVG with cell colors. Player tokens as colored circles with initials. Auto-advance through resolution steps with &#96;useEffect&#96; timers.\n- **[BattleRoyalePlayer.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/BattleRoyalePlayer.tsx)** -- Main player component. States: lobby (waiting screen), planning (Command Queue with 3 slots + D-pad + Lock In button per PRD Section 9.2), resolution (text event log), eliminated (spectator), game-over (placement + stats + Play Again). The Command Queue is the core innovation: single D-pad used 3 times sequentially (Move1 -&gt; Move2 -&gt; Shoot).\n- **[Grid.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/Grid.tsx)** -- Renders the square grid (8x8/10x10/12x12). Each cell colored by state: neutral for safe, orange pulse for warning, red for danger. Player tokens overlaid.\n- **[PlayerToken.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/PlayerToken.tsx)** -- Animated colored circle with player initial letter. Uses framer-motion for smooth position transitions.\n- **[Projectile.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/Projectile.tsx)** -- Animated projectile traveling in a direction. Colored dot with trail effect.\n- **[CommandQueue.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/CommandQueue.tsx)** -- Three horizontal slots (Move1, Move2, Shoot) showing selected directions. Active slot pulses. Tapping filled slot re-activates it.\n- **[DPad.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/DPad.tsx)** -- Cross-shaped 4-direction controller with center button (STAY/SKIP). Large 48x48px touch targets.\n- **[PlayerHUD.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/PlayerHUD.tsx)** -- Bottom bar showing all players: name, color, HP pips, ready/locked-in status.\n- **[GameOver.tsx](apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/GameOver.tsx)** -- Final results: winner/draw announcement, placement table, stats per player, Play Again + Back to Lobby buttons.\n\n### Files to Modify\n\n- **[packages/game-logic/src/index.ts](packages/game-logic/src/index.ts)** -- Add &#96;export * from './games/battle-royale'&#96;.\n- **[apps/web/app/games/page.tsx](apps/web/app/games/page.tsx)** -- Add Battle Royale &#96;GameCard&#96; to the games library grid.\n\n## Implementation Order\n\nThe steps follow the [add-new-game workflow](.agents/workflows/add-new-game.md):\n\n1. Types + logic + zone + resolution in game-logic\n2. Export from package index\n3. Approval tests\n4. Server handler\n5. Frontend pages (host/player routes)\n6. Frontend components (host display, player controller, grid, animations)\n7. Landing page + games library card\n8. Build + test\n",
```

---

All tasks are complete. Here's a summary of everything that was built:

## Battle Royale Game -- Complete

### Game Logic (`packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/`)
- **`types.ts`** -- All types: `BattleRoyaleGameState`, `BattleRoyalePlayer`, `PlayerActions`, `ArenaConfig`, `Direction`, `CellState`, `Phase`, `RoundEvent`, `ResolutionStep`, `BattleRoyaleMessage`, plus constants (`ARENA_CONFIGS`, `PLAYER_COLOR_HEX`, `START_HP`, etc.)
- **`zone.ts`** -- Zone shrink algorithm: promotes warning->danger, picks new warning cells from outermost safe ring
- **`resolution.ts`** -- `resolveRound()` produces sequential `ResolutionStep[]` for move1, move2, shoot, zone, and round-summary phases. Handles simultaneous shooting, zone damage, elimination, and win/draw detection
- **`logic.ts`** -- `createInitialBattleRoyaleState()` and `applyBattleRoyaleMessage()` reducer handling: `start_game`, `submit_actions`, `unlock_actions`, `end_planning`, `advance_phase`, `play_again`. Includes AFK detection (2 rounds of inactivity = auto-elimination)
- **27 approval tests** all passing

### Server (`apps/server/src/server.ts`)
- Added `battle-royale` to game type union, message routing, initialization, and handler. Syncs player names from room on game start.

### Frontend Pages
- **`/games/battle-royale`** -- Landing page with animated grid hero, "How to Play" steps, Host/Join buttons with device detection
- **`/games/battle-royale/host`** -- Host route with `usePartyRoom` hook
- **`/games/battle-royale/player`** -- Player route with lobby session persistence

### Frontend Components (`apps/web/components/games/battle-royale/`)
- **`BattleRoyaleHost`** -- Lobby (QR + room code + arena size/timer settings), planning (grid + countdown timer + player HUD), resolution (auto-advancing phases with delays), game-over (via `GameOver` component)
- **`BattleRoyalePlayer`** -- Lobby (waiting), planning (Command Queue + D-pad + Lock In), resolution (event log), eliminated (spectator), game-over (stats + play again)
- **`Grid`** -- Renders dynamic square grid (8x8/10x10/12x12) with cell colors and player tokens
- **`PlayerToken`** -- Animated colored circle with initial letter and HP pips, smooth framer-motion transitions
- **`DPad`** -- Cross-shaped 4-direction controller with center STAY/SKIP button
- **`CommandQueue`** -- Three horizontal slots (Move1, Move2, Shoot) with active-slot pulsing
- **`PlayerHUD`** -- Bottom bar with player names, colors, HP, and ready status
- **`GameOver`** -- Winner/draw announcement with ranked stats table

### Games Library
- Added Battle Royale `GameCard` to `/games` page

### Build Status
- `npm run build` passes (all 3 packages: game-logic, server, web)
- All 27 battle-royale approval tests pass

Vše vypadá správně. Shrnu co jsem udělal:

**Grid.tsx** -- přepsal jsem `showProjectile` (single) na `projectiles` (pole `ProjectileData[]`) s `AnimatePresence` pro plynulé vstupy/výstupy. Přidal jsem taky `hitCells` prop, který na chvíli rozsvítí buňku bíle, když je hráč trefený.

**BattleRoyaleHost.tsx** -- přidal jsem kompletní logiku pro animaci střelby:

1. Když nastane fáze `resolution-shoot`, extrahují se shoot eventy z aktuálního resolution stepu
2. Pro každý výstřel se spočítá startovní pozice (střelec) a cílová pozice (trefený hráč, nebo okraj gridu pokud minul)
3. Projektily se animují sekvenčně -- jeden za druhým, každý ~500ms
4. Při zásahu se krátce rozsvítí buňka cíle bíle (hit flash efekt)
5. Auto-advance delay pro shoot fázi se dynamicky škáluje podle počtu výstřelů, aby stihly všechny animace proběhnout
6. Banner v horní části se mění -- ukazuje barvu střelce a zda trefil

Restartni dev server a vyzkoušej -- kulky by teď měly létat od střelce daným směrem.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
There are a lot of unicode escape sequences across multiple files. The ones inside JavaScript strings (like `'\uD83D\uDC64'`, `'\u2191'`, etc.) will render correctly because they're inside JS string literals. But the ones that are directly in JSX text (not inside a string) will show as literal text.

Let me identify which ones are problematic:

1. `BattleRoyaleHost.tsx:325-327`: `'Small 8\u00D78'` - inside JS string, this is fine (renders as `×`)
2. `BattleRoyaleHost.tsx:453`: `.join(' \u2022 ')` - inside JS string, fine (bullet •)
</details></think>

Ano, je to přesně tak. Na řádku 21 je konstanta:

```21:21:packages/game-logic/src/games/battle-royale/logic.ts
const AFK_ROUNDS_TO_ELIMINATE = 2;
```

Logika funguje takhle:
- Když hráč **nesubmitne žádné akce** a **nelockne in** během planning fáze, počítadlo `afkRounds` se mu zvedne o 1
- Jakmile má hráč **2 po sobě jdoucí AFK kola**, je automaticky eliminován (HP na 0)
- Pokud hráč v jakémkoli kole odešle akce nebo lockne, počítadlo se resetuje na 0

Takže pokud hráč nepošle tahy dvě kola za sebou, hra ho vyřadí. To je záměr z PRD, aby AFK hráči neblokovali hru. Chceš to nějak změnit -- třeba zvýšit limit nebo to úplně vypnout?
